   tech = data.get('TECHNICIAN', '')\n            day = int(data.get('DAY', 0))\n            completed_str = data.get('COMPLETED', '')\n            # Convert HH:MM to minutes\n            if completed_str and ':' in completed_str:\n                h, m = completed_str.split(':')\n                completed_min = int(h) * 60 + int(m)\n            else:\n                completed_min = 0\n            started_str = data.get('STARTED', '')\n            if started_str and ':' in started_str:\n                h, m = started_str.split(':')\n                started_min = int(h) * 60 + int(m)\n            else:\n                started_min = 0\n            events.append({\n                'work_order_id': wo,\n                'machine_id': machine,\n                'technician_id': tech,\n                'day': day,\n                'started_min': started_min,\n                'completed_min': completed_min,\n            })\n    return events\n\nprint(\"Parsing service reports...\")\nservice_events = parse_service_reports()\nprint(f\"Total maintenance events: {len(service_events)}\")\n\n# Load production batches\nprint(\"Loading production batches...\")\nbatches = []\nwith open('/app/production_batches.csv') as f:\n    reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n    for row in reader:\n        batches.append({\n            'production_day': int(row['production_day']),\n            'batch_id': row['batch_id'],\n            'job_id': row['job_id'],\n            'machine_id': row['machine_id'],\n            'product_id': row['product_id'],\n            'produced_quantity': int(row['produced_quantity']),\n        })\nprint(f\"Total batches: {len(batches)}\")\n\n# Load quality inspections\nprint(\"Loading quality inspections...\")\ninspections = {}\nwith open('/app/quality_inspections.csv') as f:\n    reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n    for row in reader:\n        inspections[row['batch_id']] = {\n            'inspection_day': int(row['inspection_day']),\n            'product_id': row['product_id'],\n            'rejected_pct': float(row['rejected_pct']),\n            'defect_code': row['defect_code'],\n            'inspection_result': row['inspection_result'],\n        }\nprint(f\"Total inspections: {len(inspections)}\")\n\n# Load products (with rejection thresholds)\nproducts = {}\nwith open('/app/products.csv') as f:\n    reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n    for row in reader:\n        products[row['product_id']] = {\n            'product_family': row['product_family'],\n            'quality_rejection_threshold_pct': float(row['quality_rejection_threshold_pct']),\n        }\n\n# Find rejected batches\nrejected_batches = {}\nfor batch_id, insp in inspections.items():\n    if insp['inspection_result'] == 'REJECTED':\n        rejected_batches[batch_id] = insp\nprint(f\"Total rejected batches: {len(rejected_batches)}\")\n\n# Build maintenance timeline per machine\n# Sort events per machine by (day, completed_min)\nmachine_maintenance = defaultdict(list)\nfor ev in service_events:\n    machine_maintenance[ev['machine_id']].append(ev)\n\nfor machine_id in machine_maintenance:\n    machine_maintenance[machine_id].sort(key=lambda x: (x['day'], x['completed_min']))\n\n# For each batch, find which maintenance event it falls after\n# (batch produced after maintenance completed, before next maintenance on same machine)\n# Production batches don't have a time within day, only production_day\n# We assume all batches on a given day start at beginning of day (day boundary)\n# Maintenance completed time is within the day\n# So if maintenance completes on day D at time T, the batch produced on day D\n# would have been produced AFTER time T if it starts AFTER T, but we don't know batch start time within day.\n# The problem says \"production started after maintenance was completed\"\n# Since we have production_day but not production time, we'll assume:\n# - Batches produced on day D are produced AFTER any maintenance that completed on day D\n# - Actually we need to be more careful: if maintenance on day 5 completed at 23:59,\n#   and a batch was produced on day 5, it might have been produced before maintenance completed.\n#\n# Let's think about this differently:\n# - A maintenance event on day D completed at time T means:\n#   - Batches on day D+1 and later are definitely after this maintenance\n#   - Batches on day D may or may not be after (depends on when in day batch was produced)\n#\n# For simplicity and to be conservative, let's say a batch was produced \"after\" maintenance\n# if batch_production_day > maintenance_day OR\n# (batch_production_day == maintenance_day AND batch was produced after the maintenance time)\n# Since we don't know batch production time within day, we'll use:\n# - If maintenance completes on day D (any time), batches on day D+1 onwards are definitely after it\n# - Batches on same day D are ambiguous - we'll include them as \"after\" since maintenance\n#   typically completes early in the day and production runs throughout.\n#\n# Actually, looking at the service reports: maintenance runs in early morning (01:18 - 02:38, etc.)\n# So batches produced on the same day would be after maintenance. Let's include same-day batches.\n\n# For each batch, determine which maintenance window it's in\n# For each batch on machine M on day D:\n# Find the most recent maintenance event completed on M before (or on) day D\n# (or before D's production if same day, but we'll simplify)\n\nbatch_to_maintenance = {}\n\nfor batch in batches:\n    machine_id = batch['machine_id']\n    prod_day = batch['production_day']\n\n    events = machine_maintenance.get(machine_id, [])\n\n    # Find all maintenance events that completed before or on production day\n    # (batch production starts after maintenance completion)\n    # The \"next\" maintenance defines the end of the window\n\n    # Find the most recent maintenance event where event_day <= prod_day\n    # (since maintenance completes within its day, and batch is produced that day or later)\n\n    prev_event = None\n    next_event = None\n\n    for i, ev in enumerate(events):\n        if ev['day'] <= prod_day:\n            prev_event = ev\n        else:\n            # This event is on a day after production\n            next_event = ev\n            break\n\n    # The batch is in the window after prev_event (if any) and before next_event\n    if prev_event is not None:\n        batch_to_maintenance[batch['batch_id']] = {\n            'maintenance_event': prev_event,\n            'next_maintenance': next_event,\n        }\n\nprint(f\"Batches linked to a maintenance event: {len(batch_to_maintenance)}\")\n\n# Now find rejected batches that are linked to maintenance\nrejected_with_maintenance = []\nfor batch in batches:\n    bid = batch['batch_id']\n    if bid not in rejected_batches:\n        continue\n    if bid not in batch_to_maintenance:\n        continue\n\n    maint = batch_to_maintenance[bid]['maintenance_event']\n    rejected_with_maintenance.append({\n        'batch_id': bid,\n        'machine_id': batch['machine_id'],\n        'product_id': batch['product_id'],\n        'production_day': batch['production_day'],\n        'technician_id': maint['technician_id'],\n        'work_order_id': maint['work_order_id'],\n        'maintenance_day': maint['day'],\n    })\n\nprint(f\"Rejected batches linked to maintenance: {len(rejected_with_maintenance)}\")\n\n# Count per technician: rejected batches and work orders\ntech_batches = defaultdict(list)  # tech -> list of batch records\ntech_work_orders = defaultdict(set)  # tech -> set of work order IDs\n\nfor rec in rejected_with_maintenance:\n    tech = rec['technician_id']\n    tech_batches[tech].append(rec)\n    tech_work_orders[tech].add(rec['work_order_id'])\n\n# Apply confirmation criteria:\n# >= 3 distinct rejected batches, distributed across >= 2 different work orders\nconfirmed_techs = set()\nfor tech, batches_list in tech_batches.items():\n    n_batches = len(batches_list)\n    n_work_orders = len(tech_work_orders[tech])\n    if n_batches >= 3 and n_work_orders >= 2:\n        confirmed_techs.add(tech)\n        print(f\"CONFIRMED: {tech} - {n_batches} rejected batches, {n_work_orders} work orders\")\n\nprint(f\"\\nTotal confirmed technicians: {len(confirmed_techs)}\")\n\n# For confirmed technicians, list products affected\nproduct_to_techs = defaultdict(set)\n\nfor rec in rejected_with_maintenance:\n    if rec['technician_id'] in confirmed_techs:\n        product_to_techs[rec['product_id']].add(rec['technician_id'])\n\n# Build answer\nanswer = {}\nfor product_id in sorted(product_to_techs.keys()):\n    techs = sorted(product_to_techs[product_id])\n    answer[product_id] = techs\n\nprint(\"\\nAnswer:\")\nprint(json.dumps(answer, indent=2))\n\n# Save answer\nwith open('/app/answer.json', 'w') as f:\n    json.dump(answer, f, indent=2)\n\nprint(\"\\nAnswer saved to /app/answer.json\")\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":312,"cache_read_input_tokens":26170,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":312,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":49,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"7eb1499c-650f-4ac9-a026-11d01c0514ec","uuid":"dd6ffef7-8855-4a44-ab49-039ef2d4f744","request_id":"req_011CbfBTtb6GNqcq1QHiBccp"}

machine_maintenance:\n    machine_maintenance[machine_id].sort(key=lambda x: (x['day'], x['completed_min']))\n\n# For each batch, find which maintenance event it falls after\n# (batch produced after maintenance completed, before next maintenance on same machine)\n# Production batches don't have a time within day, only production_day\n# We assume all batches on a given day start at beginning of day (day boundary)\n# Maintenance completed time is within the day\n# So if maintenance completes on day D at time T, the batch produced on day D\n# would have been produced AFTER time T if it starts AFTER T, but we don't know batch start time within day.\n# The problem says \"production started after maintenance was completed\"\n# Since we have production_day but not production time, we'll assume:\n# - Batches produced on day D are produced AFTER any maintenance that completed on day D\n# - Actually we need to be more careful: if maintenance on day 5 completed at 23:59,\n#   and a batch was produced on day 5, it might have been produced before maintenance completed.\n#\n# Let's think about this differently:\n# - A maintenance event on day D completed at time T means:\n#   - Batches on day D+1 and later are definitely after this maintenance\n#   - Batches on day D may or may not be after (depends on when in day batch was produced)\n#\n# For simplicity and to be conservative, let's say a batch was produced \"after\" maintenance\n# if batch_production_day > maintenance_day OR\n# (batch_production_day == maintenance_day AND batch was produced after the maintenance time)\n# Since we don't know batch production time within day, we'll use:\n# - If maintenance completes on day D (any time), batches on day D+1 onwards are definitely after it\n# - Batches on same day D are ambiguous - we'll include them as \"after\" since maintenance\n#   typically completes early in the day and production runs throughout.\n#\n# Actually, looking at the service reports: maintenance runs in early morning (01:18 - 02:38, etc.)\n# So batches produced on the same day would be after maintenance. Let's include same-day batches.\n\n# For each batch, determine which maintenance window it's in\n# For each batch on machine M on day D:\n# Find the most recent maintenance event completed on M before (or on) day D\n# (or before D's production if same day, but we'll simplify)\n\nbatch_to_maintenance = {}\n\nfor batch in batches:\n    machine_id = batch['machine_id']\n    prod_day = batch['production_day']\n\n    events = machine_maintenance.get(machine_id, [])\n\n    # Find all maintenance events that completed before or on production day\n    # (batch production starts after maintenance completion)\n    # The \"next\" maintenance defines the end of the window\n\n    # Find the most recent maintenance event where event_day <= prod_day\n    # (since maintenance completes within its day, and batch is produced that day or later)\n\n    prev_event = None\n    next_event = None\n\n    for i, ev in enumerate(events):\n        if ev['day'] <= prod_day:\n            prev_event = ev\n        else:\n            # This event is on a day after production\n            next_event = ev\n            break\n\n    # The batch is in the window after prev_event (if any) and before next_event\n    if prev_event is not None:\n        batch_to_maintenance[batch['batch_id']] = {\n            'maintenance_event': prev_event,\n            'next_maintenance': next_event,\n        }\n\nprint(f\"Batches linked to a maintenance event: {len(batch_to_maintenance)}\")\n\n# Now find rejected batches that are linked to maintenance\nrejected_with_maintenance = []\nfor batch in batches:\n    bid = batch['batch_id']\n    if bid not in rejected_batches:\n        continue\n    if bid not in batch_to_maintenance:\n        continue\n\n    maint = batch_to_maintenance[bid]['maintenance_event']\n    rejected_with_maintenance.append({\n        'batch_id': bid,\n        'machine_id': batch['machine_id'],\n        'product_id': batch['product_id'],\n        'production_day': batch['production_day'],\n        'technician_id': maint['technician_id'],\n        'work_order_id': maint['work_order_id'],\n        'maintenance_day': maint['day'],\n    })\n\nprint(f\"Rejected batches linked to maintenance: {len(rejected_with_maintenance)}\")\n\n# Count per technician: rejected batches and work orders\ntech_batches = defaultdict(list)  # tech -> list of batch records\ntech_work_orders = defaultdict(set)  # tech -> set of work order IDs\n\nfor rec in rejected_with_maintenance:\n    tech = rec['technician_id']\n    tech_batches[tech].append(rec)\n    tech_work_orders[tech].add(rec['work_order_id'])\n\n# Apply confirmation criteria:\n# >= 3 distinct rejected batches, distributed across >= 2 different work orders\nconfirmed_techs = set()\nfor tech, batches_list in tech_batches.items():\n    n_batches = len(batches_list)\n    n_work_orders = len(tech_work_orders[tech])\n    if n_batches >= 3 and n_work_orders >= 2:\n        confirmed_techs.add(tech)\n        print(f\"CONFIRMED: {tech} - {n_batches} rejected batches, {n_work_orders} work orders\")\n\nprint(f\"\\nTotal confirmed technicians: {len(confirmed_techs)}\")\n\n# For confirmed technicians, list products affected\nproduct_to_techs = defaultdict(set)\n\nfor rec in rejected_with_maintenance:\n    if rec['technician_id'] in confirmed_techs:\n        product_to_techs[rec['product_id']].add(rec['technician_id'])\n\n# Build answer\nanswer = {}\nfor product_id in sorted(product_to_techs.keys()):\n    techs = sorted(product_to_techs[product_id])\n    answer[product_id] = techs\n\nprint(\"\\nAnswer:\")\nprint(json.dumps(answer, indent=2))\n\n# Save answer\nwith open('/app/answer.json', 'w') as f:\n    json.dump(answer, f, indent=2)\n\nprint(\"\\nAnswer saved to /app/answer.json\")\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false}}

- << 'EOF'\nimport csv\nimport os\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\n# Load machine assignment log with precise times\nmal = []\nwith open('/app/machine_assignment_log.csv') as f:\n    reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n    for row in reader:\n        mal.append({\n            'day': int(row['day']),\n            'job_id': row['job_id'],\n            'machine_id': row['machine_id'],\n            'sequence': int(row['sequence_on_machine']),\n            'start_time': row['start_time'],\n            'end_time': row['end_time'],\n        })\n\n# Convert time to minutes\ndef time_to_min(t):\n    h, m = t.split(':')\n    return int(h)*60 + int(m)\n\n# Load production batches\nbatches_by_job = {}\nwith open('/app/production_batches.csv') as f:\n    reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n    for row in reader:\n        batches_by_job[row['job_id']] = {\n            'batch_id': row['batch_id'],\n            'machine_id': row['machine_id'],\n            'product_id': row['product_id'],\n            'production_day': int(row['production_day']),\n        }\n\n# Load quality inspections\ninspections = {}\nwith open('/app/quality_inspections.csv') as f:\n    reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n    for row in reader:\n        inspections[row['batch_id']] = row['inspection_result']\n\n# Build a mapping from batch to production details with precise time\nbatch_details = {}\nfor job_log in mal:\n    job_id = job_log['job_id']\n    if job_id in batches_by_job:\n        b = batches_by_job[job_id]\n        batch_details[b['batch_id']] = {\n            'machine_id': b['machine_id'],\n            'product_id': b['product_id'],\n            'production_day': b['production_day'],\n            'job_id': job_id,\n            'start_min': time_to_min(job_log['start_time']),\n            'end_min': time_to_min(job_log['end_time']),\n        }\n\nprint(f\"Batch details with timing: {len(batch_details)}\")\nprint(f\"Total batches: {len(batches_by_job)}\")\n\n# Count overall rejection rate\ntotal = 0\nrejected = 0\nfor bid, result in inspections.items():\n    total += 1\n    if result == 'REJECTED':\n        rejected += 1\nprint(f\"Overall rejection rate: {rejected}/{total} = {rejected/total:.4f}\")\n\n# Per product rejection rate\nfrom collections import Counter\nproduct_batches = defaultdict(int)\nproduct_rejected = defaultdict(int)\nfor bid, details in batch_details.items():\n    if bid in inspections:\n        product_batches[details['product_id']] += 1\n        if inspections[bid] == 'REJECTED':\n            product_rejected[details['product_id']] += 1\n\nprint(\"\\nPer-product rejection rates (top 10 most rejected):\")\nrates = [(p, product_rejected[p], product_batches[p], product_rejected[p]/product_batches[p]) \n         for p in - << 'EOF'\nimport csv\nimport os\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nimport math\n\n# Parse service reports\ndef parse_service_reports():\n    events = []\n    base = '/app/raw_service_reports'\n    for day_dir in sorted(os.listdir(base)):\n        day_path = os.path.join(base, day_dir)\n        if not os.path.isdir(day_path):\n            continue\n        for fname in sorted(os.listdir(day_path)):\n            fpath = os.path.join(day_path, fname)\n            with open(fpath) as f:\n                content = f.read()\n            data = {}\n            for line in content.strip().splitlines():\n                if ':' in line:\n                    k, _, v = line.partition(':')\n                    data[k.strip()] = v.strip()\n            wo = data.get('WORK ORDER', '')\n            machine = data.get('MACHINE', '')\n            tech = data.get('TECHNICIAN', '')\n            day = int(data.get('DAY', 0))\n            completed_str = data.get('COMPLETED', '')\n            if completed_str and ':' in completed_str:\n                h, m = completed_str.split(':')\n                completed_min = int(h) * 60 + int(m)\n            else:\n                completed_min = 0\n            started_str = data.get('STARTED', '')\n            if started_str and ':' in started_str:\n                h, m = started_str.split(':')\n                started_min = int(h) * 60 + int(m)\n            else:\n                started_min = 0\n            events.append({\n                'work_order_id': wo,\n                'machine_id': machine,\n                'technician_id': tech,\n                'day': day,\n                'started_min': started_min,\n                'completed_min': completed_min,\n            })\n    return events\n\ndef time_to_min(t):\n    h, m = t.split(':')\n    return int(h)*60 + int(m)\n\nservice_events = parse_service_reports()\n\n# Load machine assignment log\nmal = {}\nwith open('/app/machine_assignment_log.csv') as f:\n    reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n    for row in reader:\n        mal[row['job_id']] = {\n            'day': int(row['day']),\n            'machine_id': row['machine_id'],\n            'start_min': time_to_min(row['start_time']),\n        }\n\n# Load production batches\nbatches = []\nwith open('/app/production_batches.csv') as f:\n    reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n    for row in reader:\n        job_id = row['job_id']\n        job_info = mal.get(job_id, {})\n        batches.append({\n            'batch_id': row['batch_id'],\n            'job_id': job_id,\n            'machine_id': row['machine_id'],\n            'product_id': row['product_id'],\n            'production_day': int(row['production_day']),\n            'start_min': job_info.get('start_min', 0),\n        })\n\n# Load quality inspections\ninspections = {}\nwith open('/app/quality_inspections.csv') as f:\n    reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n    for row in reader:\n        inspections[row['batch_id']] = row['inspection_result']\n\n# Build maintenance timeline per machine (sorted by day, then completed time)\nmachine_maintenance = defaultdict(list)\nfor ev in service_events:\n    machine_maintenance[ev['machine_id']].append(ev)\n\nfor mid in machine_maintenance:\n    machine_maintenance[mid].sort(key=lambda x: (x['day'], x['completed_min']))\n\n# For each maintenance window, compute batches in window and rejection count\n# A window is defined by: maintenance event E_i to next maintenance E_{i+1}\n# Batches in window: batch is on same machine, and batch (day, start_min) > (E_i.day, E_i.completed_min)\n# and (day, start_min) <= (E_{i+1}.day, E_{i+1}.completed_min) or no E_{i+1}\n\n# For each machine, pair each maintenance event with its subsequent window\nmachine_windows = {}  # (machine_id, event_idx) -> {event, batches: []}\nfor machine_id, events in machine_maintenance.items():\n    machine_windows[machine_id] = []\n    for i, ev in enumerate(events):\n        next_ev = events[i+1] if i+1 < len(events) else None\n        machine_windows[machine_id].append({\n            'event': ev,\n            'next_event': next_ev,\n            'batches': [],\n            'rejected': [],\n        })\n\n# Assign batches to windows\nfor batch in batches:\n    mid = batch['machine_id']\n    if mid not in machine_windows:\n        continue\n    windows = machine_windows[mid]\n    \n    # Binary search for the right window\n    # Find window where event (day, completed_min) <= batch (day, start_min) < next event\n    batch_time = (batch['production_day'], batch['start_min'])\n    \n    assigned_window = None\n    for i, win in enumerate(windows):\n        ev = win['event']\n        ev_time = (ev['day'], ev['completed_min'])\n        \n        if batch_time >= ev_time:\n            if win['next_event'] is None:\n                assigned_window = i\n            else:\n                nev = win['next_event']\n                nev_time = (nev['day'], nev['completed_min'])\n                if batch_time < nev_time:\n                    assigned_window = i\n    \n    if assigned_window is not None:\n        win = machine_windows[mid][assigned_window]\n        win['batches'].append(batch['batch_id'])\n        if inspections.get(batch['batch_id']) == 'REJECTED':\n            win['rejected'].append(batch['batch_id'])\n\n# Compute window statistics\nall_window_stats = []\nfor machine_id, windows in machine_windows.items():\n    for win in windows:\n        n = len(win['batches'])\n        r = len(win['rejected'])\n        all_window_stats.append({\n            'machine_id': machine_id,\n            'event': win['event'],\n            'n_batches': n,\n            'n_rejected': r,\n            'rejection_rate': r/n if n > 0 else 0,\n            'rejected_batches': win['rejected'],\n        })\n\n# Distribution of rejections per window\nrej_counts = defaultdict(int)\nfor ws in all_window_stats:\n    rej_counts[ws['n_rejected']] += 1\n\nprint(\"Distribution of rejected batches per maintenance window:\")\nfor k in sorted(rej_counts.keys())[:15]:\n    print(f\"  {k} rejections: {rej_counts[k]} windows\")\n\n# Find windows with most rejections\ntop_windows = sorted(all_window_stats, key=lambda x: -x['n_rejected'])[:20]\nprint(\"\\nTop 20 windows by rejection count:\")\nfor ws in top_windows:\n    ev = ws['event']\n    print(f\"  Machine {ws['machine_id']}, WO {ev['work_order_id']}, Tech {ev['technician_id']}, \"\n          f\"Day {ev['day']}, Batches: {ws['n_batches']}, Rejected: {ws['n_rejected']}, \"\n          f\"Rate: {ws['rejection_rate']:.3f}\")\nEOF","description":"Analyze per-window rejection - << 'EOF'\nimport csv\nimport os\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\ndef parse_service_reports():\n    events = []\n    base = '/app/raw_service_reports'\n    for day_dir in sorted(os.listdir(base)):\n        day_path = os.path.join(base, day_dir)\n        if not os.path.isdir(day_path):\n            continue\n        for fname in sorted(os.listdir(day_path)):\n            fpath = os.path.join(day_path, fname)\n            with open(fpath) as f:\n                content = f.read()\n            data = {}\n            for line in content.strip().splitlines():\n                if ':' in line:\n                    k, _, v = line.partition(':')\n                    data[k.strip()] = v.strip()\n            wo = data.get('WORK ORDER', '')\n            machine = data.get('MACHINE', '')\n            tech = data.get('TECHNICIAN', '')\n            day = int(data.get('DAY', 0))\n            completed_str = data.get('COMPLETED', '')\n            h, m = completed_str.split(':')\n            completed_min = int(h)*60 + int(m)\n            events.append({'work_order_id': wo, 'machine_id': machine, 'technician_id': tech,\n                          'day': day, 'completed_min': completed_min})\n    return events\n\ndef time_to_min(t):\n    h, m = t.split(':')\n    return int(h)*60 + int(m)\n\nservice_events = parse_service_reports()\n\nmal = {}\nwith open('/app/machine_assignment_log.csv') as f:\n    for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n        mal[row['job_id']] = {'day': int(row['day']), 'machine_id': row['machine_id'],\n                               'start_min': time_to_min(row['start_time'])}\n\nbatches = []\nwith open('/app/production_batches.csv') as f:\n    for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n        job_id = row['job_id']\n        ji = mal.get(job_id, {})\n        batches.append({'batch_id': row['batch_id'], 'machine_id': row['machine_id'],\n                       'product_id': row['product_id'], 'production_day': int(row['production_day']),\n                       'start_min': ji.get('start_min', 0)})\n\ninspections = {}\nwith open('/app/quality_inspections.csv') as f:\n    for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n        inspections[row['batch_id']] = row['inspection_result']\n\nmachine_maintenance = defaultdict(list)\nfor ev in service_events:\n    machine_maintenance[ev['machine_id']].append(ev)\nfor mid in machine_maintenance:\n    machine_maintenance[mid].sort(key=lambda x: (x['day'], x['completed_min']))\n\n# Build windows with detailed product breakdown\nmachine_windows = {}\nfor machine_id, events in machine_maintenance.items():\n    machine_windows[machine_id] = []\n    for i, ev in enumerate(events):\n        next_ev = events[i+1] if i+1 < len(events) else None\n        machine_windows[machine_id].append({'event': ev, 'next_event': next_ev,\n                                            'batches': [], 'rejected_batches': []})\n\nfor batch in batches:\n    mid = batch['machine_id']\n    if mid not in machine_windows:\n        continue\n    batch_time = (batch['production_day'], batch['start_min'])\n    assigned = None\n    for i, win in enumerate(machine_windows[mid]):\n        ev_time = (win['event']['day'], win['event']['completed_min'])\n        if batch_time >= ev_time:\n            if win['next_event'] is None:\n                assigned = i\n            else:\n                nev = win['next_event']\n                nev_time = (nev['day'], nev['completed_min'])\n                if batch_time < nev_time:\n                    assigned = i\n    if assigned is not None:\n        win = machine_windows[mid][assigned]\n        win['batches'].append(batch)\n        if inspections.get(batch['batch_id']) == 'REJECTED':\n            win['rejected_batches'].append(batch)\n\n# Look at specific high-rejection windows to understand product patterns\nprint(\"Detailed analysis of top rejection windows:\\n\")\ntop_windows = []\nfor mid, windows in machine_windows.items():\n    for win in windows:\n        if len(win['rejected_batches']) >= 2:\n            top_windows.append(win)\n\ntop_windows.sort(key=lambda x: -len(x['rejected_batches']))\n\nfor win in top_windows[:20]:\n    ev = win['event']\n    print(f\"Machine {ev['machine_id']}, WO {ev['work_order_id']}, Tech {ev['technician_id']}, Day {ev['day']}\")\n    print(f\"  Window: {len(win['batches'])} batches, {len(win['rejected_batches'])} rejected\")\n    \n    # Product breakdown\n    prod_total = defaultdict(int)\n    prod_rej = defaultdict(int)\n    for b in win['batches']:\n        prod_total[b['product_id']] += 1\n    for b in win['rejected_batches']:\n        prod_rej[b['product_id']] += 1\n    \n    for prod, rej in sorted(prod_rej.items()):\n        tot = prod_total[prod]\n        print(f\"    {prod}: {rej}/{tot} rejected in window\")\n    print()\nEOF","description":"Analyze product breakdown in high-rejection   if i > 0:\n            coeff *= (n - i + 1) / i\n        result += coeff * (p ** i) * ((1-p) ** (n-i))\n    return min(result, 1.0)\n\ndef binom_pvalue(k, n, p):\n    \"\"\"P(X >= k | n, p) - one-sided p-value for elevated rejection\"\"\"\n    return 1.0 - binom_cdf(k-1, n, p)\n\nprint(\"Loading data...\")\nservice_events = parse_service_reports()\n\nmal = {}\nwith 'start_min': time_to_min(row['start_time'])}\n\nbatches = []\nwith open('/app/production_batches.csv') as f:\n    for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n        ji = mal.get(row['job_id'], {})\n        batches.append({'batch_id': row['batch_id'], 'machine_id': row['machine_id'],\n                       'product_id': row['product_id'], 'production_day': int(row['production_day']),\n machine_windows[machine_id] = []\n    for i, ev in enumerate(events):\n        next_ev = events[i+1] if i+1 < len(events) else None\n        machine_windows[machine_id].append({\n            'event': batch_time = (batch['production_day'], batch['start_min'])\n    assigned = None\n    for i, win in enumerate(machine_windows[mid]):\n        ev_time = (win['event']['day'],   if i > 0:\n            coeff *= (n - i + 1) / i\n        result += coeff * (p ** i) * ((1-p) ** (n-i))\n    return min(result, 1.0)\n\ndef binom_pvalue(k, n, p):\n    \"\"\"P(X >= k | n, p) - 'start_min': time_to_min(row['start_time'])}\n\nbatches = []\nwith open('/app/production_batches.csv') as f:\n    for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n        ji = mal.get(row['job_id'], {})\n        batches.append({'batch_id': row['batch_id'], 'machine_id': row['machine_id'],\n                       'product_id': row['product_id'], 'production_day': int(row['production_day']),\n Build maintenance timeline per machine\nmachine_maintenance = defaultdict(list)\nfor ev in service_events:\n    machine_maintenance[ev['machine_id']].append(ev)\nfor mid in machine_maintenance:\n machine_windows[machine_id] = []\n    for i, ev in enumerate(events):\n        next_ev = events[i+1] if i+1 < len(events) else None\n        machine_windows[machine_id].append({\n            'event': ev, 'next_event': next_ev, 'batches': [], 'rejected_batches': []\n        })\n\nfor batch in batches:\n    mid = batch['machine_id']\n    if mid not in machine_windows:\n        continue\n    batch_time = (batch['production_day'], batch['start_min'])\n    assigned = None\n    for i, win in enumerate(machine_windows[mid]):\n        ev_time = (win['event']['day'], - << 'EOF'\nimport csv\nimport os\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\ndef parse_service_reports():\n    events = []\n    base = '/app/raw_service_reports'\n    for day_dir in sorted(os.listdir(base)):\n        day_path = os.path.join(base, day_dir)\n        if not os.path.isdir(day_path):\n            continue\n        for fname in sorted(os.listdir(day_path)):\n            fpath = os.path.join(day_path, fname)\n            with open(fpath) as f:\n                content = f.read()\n            data = {}\n            for line in content.strip().splitlines():\n                if ':' in line:\n                    k, _, v = line.partition(':')\n                    data[k.strip()] = v.strip()\n            completed_str = data.get('COMPLETED', '')\n            h, m = completed_str.split(':')\n            events.append({\n                'work_order_id': data.get('WORK ORDER', ''),\n                'machine_id': data.get('MACHINE', ''),\n                'technician_id': data.get('TECHNICIAN', ''),\n                'day': int(data.get('DAY', 0)),\n                'completed_min': int(h)*60 + int(m),\n            })\n    return events\n\ndef time_to_min(t):\n    h, m = t.split(':')\n    return int(h)*60 + int(m)\n\ndef binom_cdf(k, n, p):\n    result = 0.0\n    coeff = 1.0\n    for i in range(k+1):\n        if i > 0:\n            coeff *= (n - i + 1) / i\n        result += coeff * (p ** i) * ((1-p) ** (n-i))\n    return min(result, 1.0)\n\ndef binom_pvalue(k, n, p):\n    return 1.0 - binom_cdf(k-1, n, p)\n\nservice_events = parse_service_reports()\n\nmal = {}\nwith open('/app/machine_assignment_log.csv') as f:\n    for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n        mal[row['job_id']] = {'day': int(row['day']), 'machine_id': row['machine_id'],\n                               'start_min': time_to_min(row['start_time'])}\n\nbatches = []\nwith open('/app/production_batches.csv') as f:\n    for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n        ji = mal.get(row['job_id'], {})\n        batches.append({'batch_id': row['batch_id'], 'machine_id': row['machine_id'],\n                       'product_id': row['product_id'], 'production_day': int(row['production_day']),\n                       'start_min': ji.get('start_min', 0)})\n\ninspections = {}\nwith open('/app/quality_inspections.csv') as f:\n    for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n        inspections[row['batch_id']] = row['inspection_result']\n\nproduct_total = defaultdict(int)\nproduct_rejected = defaultdict(int)\nfor b in batches:\n    product_total[b['product_id']] += 1\n    if inspections.get(b['batch_id']) == 'REJECTED':\n        product_rejected[b['product_id']] += 1\nproduct_baseline = {p: product_rejected[p]/product_total[p] for p in product_total}\n\nmachine_maintenance = defaultdict(list)\nfor ev in service_events:\n    machine_maintenance[ev['machine_id']].append(ev)\nfor mid in machine_maintenance:\n    machine_maintenance[mid].sort(key=lambda x: (x['day'], x['completed_min']))\n\nmachine_windows = {}\nfor machine_id, events in machine_maintenance.items():\n    machine_windows[machine_id] = []\n    for i, ev in enumerate(events):\n        next_ev = events[i+1] if i+1 < len(events) else None\n        machine_windows[machine_id].append({\n            'event': ev, 'next_event': next_ev, 'batches': [], 'rejected_batches': []\n        })\n\nfor batch in batches:\n    mid = batch['machine_id']\n    if mid not in machine_windows:\n        continue\n    batch_time = (batch['production_day'], batch['start_min'])\n    assigned = None\n    for i, win in enumerate(machine_windows[mid]):\n        ev_time = (win['event']['day'], win['event']['completed_min'])\n        if batch_time >= ev_time:\n            if win['next_event'] is None:\n                assigned = i\n            else:\n                nev_time = (win['next_event']['day'], win['next_event']['completed_min'])\n                if batch_time < nev_time:\n                    assigned = i\n    if assigned is not None:\n        win = machine_windows[mid][assigned]\n        win['batches'].append(batch)\n        if inspections.get(batch['batch_id']) == 'REJECTED':\n            win['rejected_batches'].append(batch)\n\n# Collect all significant window-product pairs with their stats\nall_pairs = []\nfor machine_id, windows in machine_windows.items():\n    for win in windows:\n        if not win['rejected_batches']:\n            continue\n        ev = win['event']\n        prod_batches = defaultdict(list)\n        prod_rejected = defaultdict(list)\n        for b in win['batches']:\n            prod_batches[b['product_id']].append(b)\n        for b in win['rejected_batches']:\n            prod_rejected[b['product_id']].append(b)\n        for product_id, rej_list in prod_rejected.items():\n            n = len(prod_batches[product_id])\n            k = len(rej_list)\n            p_baseline = product_baseline.get(product_id, 0.01)\n            pval = binom_pvalue(k, n, p_baseline)\n            rate_in_window = k/n\n            all_pairs.append({\n                'machine_id': machine_id,\n                'technician_id': ev['technician_id'],\n                'work_order_id': ev['work_order_id'],\n                'product_id': product_id,\n                'n': n, 'k': k,\n                'rate': rate_in_window,\n                'baseline': p_baseline,\n                'fold_change': rate_in_window / p_baseline,\n                'pval': pval,\n                'batches': rej_list,\n            })\n\n# Test different thresholds\nfor threshold in [0.05, 0.01, 0.001]:\n    print(f\"\\n=== THRESHOLD p < {threshold} ===\")\n    confirmed_techs = set()\n    tech_batches = defaultdict(list)\n    tech_work_orders = defaultdict(set)\n    \n    for pair in all_pairs:\n        if pair['pval'] < threshold:\n            tech = pair['technician_id']\n            for b in pair['batches']:\n                tech_batches[tech].append({'batch_id': b['batch_id'], 'product_id': pair['product_id'],\n                                           'work_order_id': pair['work_order_id']})\n            tech_work_orders[tech].add(pair['work_order_id'])\n    \n    for tech in sorted(tech_batches.keys()):\n        n_b = len(tech_batches[tech])\n        n_wo = len(tech_work_orders[tech])\n        if n_b >= 3 and n_wo >= 2:\n            confirmed_techs.add(tech)\n    \n    print(f\"Confirmed technicians ({len(confirmed_techs)}): {sorted(confirmed_techs)}\")\n    \n    # Products\n    product_to_techs = defaultdict(set)\n    for pair in all_pairs:\n        if pair['pval'] < threshold and pair['technician_id'] in confirmed_techs:\n            product_to_techs[pair['product_id']].add(pair['technician_id'])\n    \n    for prod in sorted(product_to_techs.keys()):\n        print(f\"  {prod}: {sorted(product_to_techs[prod])}\")\n\n# Show borderline pairs (0.01 < p < 0.05)\nprint(\"\\n=== Borderline pairs (0.01 < p < 0.05) ===\")\nborderline = [(p['technician_id'], p['work_order_id'], p['product_id'], \n               p['n'], p['k'], p['rate'], p['baseline'], p['fold_change'], p['pval'])\n              for p in all_pairs if 0.01 <= p['pval'] < 0.05]\nborderline.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])\nfor tech, wo, prod, n, k, rate, base, fold, pval in borderline:\n    print(f\"  {tech}, {wo}, {prod}: {k}/{n} ({rate:.0%} vs {base:.1%} baseline, {fold:.1f}x fold, p={pval:.4f})\")\nEOF","description":"Test different p-value thresholds and show borderline - << 'EOF'\nimport csv\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\n# Load daily factory conditions\ndaily_conditions = {}\nwith open('/app/daily_factory_conditions.csv') as f:\n    for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n        daily_conditions[int(row['day'])] = {\n            'load': float(row['load_index']),\n            'humidity': float(row['humidity_index']),\n            'material': float(row['material_variation_index']),\n            'power': float(row['power_stability_index']),\n        }\n\n# Load production batches and quality inspections\nbatches = {}\nwith open('/app/production_batches.csv') as f:\n    for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n        batches[row['batch_id']] = {'production_day': int(row['production_day']), \n                                    'product_id': row['product_id'], 'machine_id': row['machine_id']}\n\ninspections = {}\nwith open('/app/quality_inspections.csv') as f:\n    for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n        inspections[row['batch_id']] = {'result': row['inspection_result'],\n                                         'inspection_day': int(row['inspection_day']),\n                                         'defect_code': row['defect_code']}\n\n# Compare environmental conditions for rejected vs accepted batches\nrejected_conditions = []\naccepted_conditions = []\n\nfor bid, insp in inspections.items():\n    if bid not in batches:\n        continue\n    prod_day = batches[bid]['production_day']\n    cond = daily_conditions.get(prod_day, {})\n    if not cond:\n        continue\n    entry = [cond['load'], cond['humidity'], cond['material'], cond['power']]\n    if insp['result'] == 'REJECTED':\n        rejected_conditions.append(entry)\n    else:\n        accepted_conditions.append(entry)\n\ndef mean(lst, idx):\n    return sum(x[idx] for x in lst) / len(lst) if lst else 0\n\ndef std(lst, idx):\n    m = mean(lst, idx)\n    return (sum((x[idx]-m)**2 for x in lst)/len(lst))**0.5 if lst else 0\n\nprint(\"Environmental conditions comparison (rejected vs accepted):\")\nlabels = ['load', 'humidity', 'material', 'power']\nfor i, label in enumerate(labels):\n    rej_mean = mean(rejected_conditions, i)\n    acc_mean = mean(accepted_conditions, i)\n    rej_std = std(rejected_conditions, i)\n    acc_std = std(accepted_conditions, i)\n    print(f\"  {label}: rejected={rej_mean:.4f}±{rej_std:.4f}, accepted={acc_mean:.4f}±{acc_std:.4f}\")\n\n# Check if there are extreme condition days with many rejections\nprint(\"\\nRejection counts by day (top rejection days):\")\nday_rejections = defaultdict(int)\nday_totals = defaultdict(int)\nfor bid, insp in inspections.items():\n    if bid not in batches:\n        continue\n    prod_day = batches[bid]['production_day']\n    day_totals[prod_day] += 1\n    if insp['result'] == 'REJECTED':\n        day_rejections[prod_day] += 1\n\ntop_days = sorted(day_rejections.keys(), key=lambda d: -day_rejections[d])[:15]\nfor day in top_days:\n    cond = daily_conditions.get(day, {})\n    rej = day_rejections[day]\n    tot = day_totals[day]\n    print(f\"  Day {day}: {rej}/{tot} rejected ({100*rej/tot:.1f}%), \"\n          f\"load={cond.get('load',0):.3f}, humidity={cond.get('humidity',0):.3f}, \"\n          f\"material={cond.get('material',0):.3f}, power={cond.get('power',0):.3f}\")\n    \n# Defect codes analysis\nprint(\"\\nDefect code distribution (all rejections):\")\ndefect_counts = defaultdict(int)\nfor bid, insp in inspections.items():\n    if insp['result'] == 'REJECTED':\n        defect_counts[insp['defect_code']] += 1\nfor code, count in sorted(defect_counts.items()):\n    print(f\"  {code}: {count}\")\nEOF","description":"Analyze << 'EOF'\nimport csv\nimport os\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\n# Count total work orders per technician from service reports\ntech_wo_count = defaultdict(set)\nbase = '/app/raw_service_reports'\nfor day_dir in sorted(os.listdir(base)):\n    day_path = os.path.join(base, day_dir)\n    if not os.path.isdir(day_path):\n        continue\n    for fname in sorted(os.listdir(day_path)):\n        fpath = os.path.join(day_path, fname)\n        with open(fpath) as f:\n            content = f.read()\n        data = {}\n        for line in content.strip().splitlines():\n            if ':' in line:\n                k, _, v = line.partition(':')\n                data[k.strip()] = v.strip()\n        tech = data.get('TECHNICIAN', '')\n        wo = data.get('WORK ORDER', '')\n        tech_wo_count[tech].add(wo)\n\nprint(\"Total work orders per technician:\")\nfor tech in sorted(tech_wo_count.keys()):\n    print(f\"  {tech}: {len(tech_wo_count[tech])} total WOs\")\n\n# For the borderline-only confirmed technicians, compute false-positive rate\n# if they have N total WOs, probability of seeing k significant WOs by chance\n# (using p=0.05 significance threshold, assuming H0 true for all)\nimport math\n\ndef binom_pvalue_atleast_k(k, n, p):\n    \"\"\"P(X >= k | n, p)\"\"\"\n    result = 0.0\n    coeff = 1.0\n    for i in range(n+1):\n        if i > 0:\n            coeff *= (n - i + 1) / i\n        term = coeff * (p**i) * ((1-p)**(n-i))\n        if i >= k:\n            result += term\n    return result\n\nprint(\"\\nProbability of false positive for borderline technicians:\")\nprint(\"(P(seeing >= X significant WOs by chance, assuming alpha=0.05 per WO))\")\nborderline_techs = {\n    'TECH_006': 3, 'TECH_008': 3, 'TECH_016': 3, \n    'TECH_019': 6, 'TECH_028': 2, 'TECH_034': 3, 'TECH_039': 3\n}\nfor tech, n_sig in sorted(borderline_techs.items()):\n    n_total = len(tech_wo_count[tech])\n    prob = binom_pvalue_atleast_k(n_sig, n_total, 0.05)\n    print(f\"  {tech}: {n_sig} significant WOs out of {n_total} total, P(false positive) = {prob:.6f}\")\nEOF","description":"Check total work orders per technician and false positive rates"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":5519,"cache_read_input_tokens":80187,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":5519,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":29,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"7eb1499c-650f-4ac9-a026-11d01c0514ec","uuid":"90c6d761-747e-422f-b463-67d344e7f04b","request_id":"req_011CbfCEiY6qYUX7BYDBpgP4"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01Gw3LQruRGF5n9A35yv6UX6","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01WdDnqKc13qTo7tGCbgqUXH","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/analyze_v3.py","content":"\"\"\"\nAnalysis approach:\n1. For each (technician, product) pair, collect ALL batches of that product\n   produced in maintenance windows following that technician's work.\n2. Test if the rejection rate in those windows is significantly elevated\n   vs. product baseline, using a binomial test.\n3. If a technician shows significantly elevated rejection rates for a product,\n   they may be responsible for maintenance-caused quality issues on that product.\n4. Apply confirmation criteria: >= 3 rejected batches across >= 2 work orders.\n5. For confirmed technicians, list all affected products.\n\nThis approach avoids per-window multiple testing issues by aggregating across\nall work orders for each (tech, product) pair.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport csv\nimport os\nimport json\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\ndef parse_service_reports():\n    events = []\n    base = '/app/raw_service_reports'\n    for day_dir in sorted(os.listdir(base)):\n        day_path = os.path.join(base, day_dir)\n        if not os.path.isdir(day_path):\n            continue\n        for fname in sorted(os.listdir(day_path)):\n            fpath = os.path.join(day_path, fname)\n            with open(fpath) as f:\n                content = f.read()\n            data = {}\n            for line in content.strip().splitlines():\n                if ':' in line:\n                    k, _, v = line.partition(':')\n                    data[k.strip()] = v.strip()\n            completed_str = data.get('COMPLETED', '')\n            h, m = completed_str.split(':')\n            events.append({\n                'work_order_id': data.get('WORK ORDER', ''),\n                'machine_id': data.get('MACHINE', ''),\n                'technician_id': data.get('TECHNICIAN', ''),\n                'day': int(data.get('DAY', 0)),\n                'completed_min': int(h)*60 + int(m),\n            })\n    return events\n\ndef time_to_min(t):\n    h, m = t.split(':')\n    return int(h)*60 + int(m)\n\ndef binom_pvalue(k, n, p):\n    \"\"\"P(X >= k | Binomial(n, p)) - one-sided p-value for elevated rate\"\"\"\n    if k <= 0:\n        return 1.0\n    result = 0.0\n    coeff = 1.0\n    for i in range(n+1):\n        if i > 0:\n            coeff *= (n - i + 1) / i\n        term = coeff * (p**i) * ((1-p)**(n-i))\n        if i >= k:\n            result += term\n    return min(result, 1.0)\n\nprint(\"Loading data...\")\nservice_events = parse_service_reports()\n\nmal = {}\nwith open('/app/machine_assignment_log.csv') as f:\n    for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n        mal[row['job_id']] = {\n            'day': int(row['day']),\n            'machine_id': row['machine_id'],\n            'start_min': time_to_min(row['start_time'])\n        }\n\nbatches = []\nwith open('/app/production_batches.csv') as f:\n    for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n        ji = mal.get(row['job_id'], {})\n        batches.append({\n            'batch_id': row['batch_id'],\n            'machine_id': row['machine_id'],\n            'product_id': row['product_id'],\n            'production_day': int(row['production_day']),\n            'start_min': ji.get('start_min', 0)\n        })\n\ninspections = {}\nwith open('/app/quality_inspections.csv') as f:\n    for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n        inspections[row['batch_id']] = row['inspection_result']\n\n# Product baseline rejection rates\nproduct_total = defaultdict(int)\nproduct_rejected = defaultdict(int)\nfor b in batches:\n    product_total[b['product_id']] += 1\n    if inspections.get(b['batch_id']) == 'REJECTED':\n        product_rejected[b['product_id']] += 1\nproduct_baseline = {p: product_rejected[p]/product_total[p] for p in product_total}\n\n# Build maintenance timeline per machine\nmachine_maintenance = defaultdict(list)\nfor ev in service_events:\n    machine_maintenance[ev['machine_id']].append(ev)\nfor mid in machine_maintenance:\n    machine_maintenance[mid].sort(key=lambda x: (x['day'], x['completed_min']))\n\n# Build windows and assign batches with precise timing\nmachine_windows = {}\nfor machine_id, events in machine_maintenance.items():\n    machine_windows[machine_id] = []\n    for i, ev in enumerate(events):\n        next_ev = events[i+1] if i+1 < len(events) else None\n        machine_windows[machine_id].append({\n            'event': ev, 'next_event': next_ev, 'batches': [], 'rejected_batches': []\n        })\n\nfor batch in batches:\n    mid = batch['machine_id']\n    if mid not in machine_windows:\n        continue\n    batch_time = (batch['production_day'], batch['start_min'])\n    assigned = None\n    for i, win in enumerate(machine_windows[mid]):\n        ev_time = (win['event']['day'], win['event']['completed_min'])\n        if batch_time >= ev_time:\n            if win['next_event'] is None:\n                assigned = i\n            else:\n                nev_time = (win['next_event']['day'], win['next_event']['completed_min'])\n                if batch_time < nev_time:\n                    assigned = i\n    if assigned is not None:\n        win = machine_windows[mid][assigned]\n        win['batches'].append(batch)\n        if inspections.get(batch['batch_id']) == 'REJECTED':\n            win['rejected_batches'].append(batch)\n\n# For each (technician, product) pair, aggregate stats across all their work orders\n# For each rejected batch in a window, track which (tech, product, work_order) it belongs to\n\n# Structure: (tech, product) -> {'total_n': int, 'total_k': int, 'rejected_batches_by_wo': {wo: [batch_ids]}}\ntech_product_stats = defaultdict(lambda: {'total_n': 0, 'total_k': 0, 'rejected_by_wo': defaultdict(list)})\n\nfor machine_id, windows in machine_windows.items():\n    for win in windows:\n        ev = win['event']\n        tech = ev['technician_id']\n        wo = ev['work_order_id']\n\n        # Count batches per product in this window\n        prod_n = defaultdict(int)\n        for b in win['batches']:\n            prod_n[b['product_id']] += 1\n\n        # Count rejected batches per product in this window\n        prod_k = defaultdict(list)\n        for b in win['rejected_batches']:\n            prod_k[b['product_id']].append(b['batch_id'])\n\n        # Add to tech-product stats\n        for product_id in prod_n:\n            key = (tech, product_id)\n            tech_product_stats[key]['total_n'] += prod_n[product_id]\n            if product_id in prod_k:\n                tech_product_stats[key]['total_k'] += len(prod_k[product_id])\n                tech_product_stats[key]['rejected_by_wo'][wo].extend(prod_k[product_id])\n\n# For each (tech, product) pair, compute p-value\n# A pair is \"significant\" if the overall rejection rate for that tech's windows is elevated\nTECH_PRODUCT_PVALUE_THRESHOLD = 0.01  # stricter threshold at aggregated level\n\nsignificant_pairs = []  # (tech, product, total_n, total_k, p_val)\nfor (tech, product), stats in tech_product_stats.items():\n    n = stats['total_n']\n    k = stats['total_k']\n    if k == 0:\n        continue\n    baseline = product_baseline.get(product, 0.01)\n    pval = binom_pvalue(k, n, baseline)\n    if pval < TECH_PRODUCT_PVALUE_THRESHOLD:\n        significant_pairs.append({\n            'technician_id': tech,\n            'product_id': product,\n            'total_n': n,\n            'total_k': k,\n            'baseline': baseline,\n            'fold_change': (k/n)/baseline,\n            'pval': pval,\n            'rejected_by_wo': dict(stats['rejected_by_wo']),\n        })\n\nprint(f\"\\nSignificant (tech, product) pairs at p<{TECH_PRODUCT_PVALUE_THRESHOLD}: {len(significant_pairs)}\")\n\n# Now apply confirmation criteria:\n# Tech is confirmed if they have >= 3 rejected batches across >= 2 DISTINCT work orders\n# (across all their significant product pairs combined)\n\n# For each confirmed pair, check work orders\ntech_total_rejected = defaultdict(list)  # tech -> list of (batch_id, wo, product)\ntech_significant_products = defaultdict(set)\n\nfor pair in significant_pairs:\n    tech = pair['technician_id']\n    product = pair['product_id']\n    for wo, batch_ids in pair['rejected_by_wo'].items():\n        for bid in batch_ids:\n            tech_total_rejected[tech].append({\n                'batch_id': bid,\n                'work_order_id': wo,\n                'product_id': product\n            })\n    tech_significant_products[tech].add(product)\n\n# Confirmation: >= 3 distinct rejected batches across >= 2 distinct work orders\nconfirmed_techs = set()\ntech_summary = {}\nfor tech, records in tech_total_rejected.items():\n    n_batches = len(records)\n    n_wos = len(set(r['work_order_id'] for r in records))\n    tech_summary[tech] = {'n_batches': n_batches, 'n_wos': n_wos}\n    if n_batches >= 3 and n_wos >= 2:\n        confirmed_techs.add(tech)\n\nprint(\"\\n--- Technician Summary (significant at tech-product level) ---\")\nfor tech in sorted(tech_summary.keys()):\n    s = tech_summary[tech]\n    status = \"CONFIRMED\" if tech in confirmed_techs else \"NOT CONFIRMED\"\n    print(f\"  {tech}: {s['n_batches']} batches, {s['n_wos']} WOs - {status}\")\n\nprint(f\"\\nTotal confirmed technicians: {len(confirmed_techs)}\")\n\n# For confirmed technicians, list affected products\n# A product is included if the (tech, product) pair is significant AND the tech is confirmed\nproduct_to_techs = defaultdict(set)\nfor pair in significant_pairs:\n    tech = pair['technician_id']\n    if tech in confirmed_techs:\n        product_to_techs[pair['product_id']].add(tech)\n\n# Build answer\nanswer = {}\nfor product_id in sorted(product_to_techs.keys()):\n    techs = sorted(product_to_techs[product_id])\n    answer[product_id] = techs\n\nprint(\"\\n=== ANSWER ===\")\nprint(json.dumps(answer, indent=2))\n\n# Detailed view per confirmed tech\nprint(\"\\n--- Detailed confirmed tech analysis ---\")\nfor tech in sorted(confirmed_techs):\n    s = tech_summary[tech]\n    print(f\"\\n{tech}: {s['n_batches']} batches, {s['n_wos']} WOs\")\n    # Show per-product pair stats\n    tech_pairs = [p for p in significant_pairs if p['technician_id'] == tech]\n    for pair in sorted(tech_pairs, key=lambda x: x['pval']):\n        wos = list(pair['rejected_by_wo'].keys())\n        total_k = pair['total_k']\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01WdDnqKc13qTo7tGCbgqUXH","type":"tool_result","content":"File created successfully at: /app/analyze_v3.py (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"7eb1499c-650f-4ac9-a026-11d01c0514ec","uuid":"caa6c010-4f7c-4d89-8a3a-248e31b99924","timestamp":"2026-06-02T22:11:41.665Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"create","filePath":"/app/analyze_v3.py","content":"\"\"\"\nAnalysis approach:\n1. For each (technician, product) pair, collect ALL batches of that product\n   produced in maintenance windows following that technician's work.\n2. Test if the rejection rate in those windows is significantly elevated\n   vs. product baseline, using a binomial test.\n3. If a technician shows significantly elevated rejection rates for a product,\n   they may be responsible for maintenance-caused quality issues on that product.\n4. Apply confirmation criteria: >= 3 rejected batches across >= 2 work orders.\n5. For confirmed technicians, list all affected products.\n\nThis approach avoids per-window multiple testing issues by aggregating across\nall work orders for each (tech, product) pair.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport csv\nimport os\nimport json\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\ndef parse_service_reports():\n    events = []\n    base = '/app/raw_service_reports'\n    for day_dir in sorted(os.listdir(base)):\n        day_path = os.path.join(base, day_dir)\n        if not os.path.isdir(day_path):\n            continue\n        for fname in sorted(os.listdir(day_path)):\n            fpath = os.path.join(day_path, fname)\n            with open(fpath) as f:\n                content = f.read()\n            data = {}\n            for line in content.strip().splitlines():\n                if ':' in line:\n                    k, _, v = line.partition(':')\n                    data[k.strip()] = v.strip()\n            completed_str = data.get('COMPLETED', '')\n            h, m = completed_str.split(':')\n            events.append({\n                'work_order_id': data.get('WORK ORDER', ''),\n                'machine_id': data.get('MACHINE', ''),\n                'technician_id': data.get('TECHNICIAN', ''),\n                'day': int(data.get('DAY', 0)),\n                'completed_min': int(h)*60 + int(m),\n            })\n    return events\n\ndef time_to_min(t):\n    h, m = t.split(':')\n    return int(h)*60 + int(m)\n\ndef binom_pvalue(k, n, p):\n    \"\"\"P(X >= k | Binomial(n, p)) - one-sided p-value for elevated rate\"\"\"\n    if k <= 0:\n        return 1.0\n    result = 0.0\n    coeff = 1.0\n    for i in range(n+1):\n        if i > 0:\n            coeff *= (n - i + 1) / i\n        term = coeff * (p**i) * ((1-p)**(n-i))\n        if i >= k:\n            result += term\n    return min(result, 1.0)\n\nprint(\"Loading data...\")\nservice_events = parse_service_reports()\n\nmal = {}\nwith open('/app/machine_assignment_log.csv') as f:\n    for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n        mal[row['job_id']] = {\n            'day': int(row['day']),\n            'machine_id': row['machine_id'],\n            'start_min': time_to_min(row['start_time'])\n        }\n\nbatches = []\nwith open('/app/production_batches.csv') as f:\n    for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n        ji = mal.get(row['job_id'], {})\n        batches.append({\n            'batch_id': row['batch_id'],\n            'machine_id': row['machine_id'],\n            'product_id': row['product_id'],\n            'production_day': int(row['production_day']),\n            'start_min': ji.get('start_min', 0)\n        })\n\ninspections = {}\nwith open('/app/quality_inspections.csv') as f:\n    for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n        inspections[row['batch_id']] = row['inspection_result']\n\n# Product baseline rejection rates\nproduct_total = defaultdict(int)\nproduct_rejected = defaultdict(int)\nfor b in batches:\n    product_total[b['product_id']] += 1\n    if inspections.get(b['batch_id']) == 'REJECTED':\n        product_rejected[b['product_id']] += 1\nproduct_baseline = {p: product_rejected[p]/product_total[p] for p in product_total}\n\n# Build maintenance timeline per machine\nmachine_maintenance = defaultdict(list)\nfor ev in service_events:\n    machine_maintenance[ev['machine_id']].append(ev)\nfor mid in machine_maintenance:\n    machine_maintenance[mid].sort(key=lambda x: (x['day'], x['completed_min']))\n\n# Build windows and assign batches with precise timing\nmachine_windows = {}\nfor machine_id, events in machine_maintenance.items():\n    machine_windows[machine_id] = []\n    for i, ev in enumerate(events):\n        next_ev = events[i+1] if i+1 < len(events) else None\n        machine_windows[machine_id].append({\n            'event': ev, 'next_event': next_ev, 'batches': [], 'rejected_batches': []\n        })\n\nfor batch in batches:\n    mid = batch['machine_id']\n    if mid not in machine_windows:\n        continue\n    batch_time = (batch['production_day'], batch['start_min'])\n    assigned = None\n    for i, win in enumerate(machine_windows[mid]):\n        ev_time = (win['event']['day'], win['event']['completed_min'])\n        if batch_time >= ev_time:\n            if win['next_event'] is None:\n                assigned = i\n            else:\n                nev_time = (win['next_event']['day'], win['next_event']['completed_min'])\n                if batch_time < nev_time:\n                    assigned = i\n    if assigned is not None:\n        win = machine_windows[mid][assigned]\n        win['batches'].append(batch)\n        if inspections.get(batch['batch_id']) == 'REJECTED':\n            win['rejected_batches'].append(batch)\n\n# For each (technician, product) pair, aggregate stats across all their work orders\n# For each rejected batch in a window, track which (tech, product, work_order) it belongs to\n\n# Structure: (tech, product) -> {'total_n': int, 'total_k': int, 'rejected_batches_by_wo': {wo: [batch_ids]}}\ntech_product_stats = defaultdict(lambda: {'total_n': 0, 'total_k': 0, 'rejected_by_wo': defaultdict(list)})\n\nfor machine_id, windows in machine_windows.items():\n    for win in windows:\n        ev = win['event']\n        tech = ev['technician_id']\n        wo = ev['work_order_id']\n\n        # Count batches per product in this window\n        prod_n = defaultdict(int)\n        for b in win['batches']:\n            prod_n[b['product_id']] += 1\n\n        # Count rejected batches per product in this window\n        prod_k = defaultdict(list)\n        for b in win['rejected_batches']:\n            prod_k[b['product_id']].append(b['batch_id'])\n\n        # Add to tech-product stats\n        for product_id in prod_n:\n            key = (tech, product_id)\n            tech_product_stats[key]['total_n'] += prod_n[product_id]\n            if product_id in prod_k:\n                tech_product_stats[key]['total_k'] += len(prod_k[product_id])\n                tech_product_stats[key]['rejected_by_wo'][wo].extend(prod_k[product_id])\n\n# For each (tech, product) pair, compute p-value\n# A pair is \"significant\" if the overall rejection rate for that tech's windows is elevated\nTECH_PRODUCT_PVALUE_THRESHOLD = 0.01  # stricter threshold at aggregated level\n\nsignificant_pairs = []  # (tech, product, total_n, total_k, p_val)\nfor (tech, product), stats in tech_product_stats.items():\n    n = stats['total_n']\n    k = stats['total_k']\n    if k == 0:\n        continue\n    baseline = product_baseline.get(product, 0.01)\n    pval = binom_pvalue(k, n, baseline)\n    if pval < TECH_PRODUCT_PVALUE_THRESHOLD:\n        significant_pairs.append({\n            'technician_id': tech,\n            'product_id': product,\n            'total_n': n,\n            'total_k': k,\n            'baseline': baseline,\n            'fold_change': (k/n)/baseline,\n            'pval': pval,\n            'rejected_by_wo': dict(stats['rejected_by_wo']),\n        })\n\nprint(f\"\\nSignificant (tech, product) pairs at p<{TECH_PRODUCT_PVALUE_THRESHOLD}: {len(significant_pairs)}\")\n\n# Now apply confirmation criteria:\n# Tech is confirmed if they have >= 3 rejected batches across >= 2 DISTINCT work orders\n# (across all their significant product pairs combined)\n\n# For each confirmed pair, check work orders\ntech_total_rejected = defaultdict(list)  # tech -> list of (batch_id, wo, product)\ntech_significant_products = defaultdict(set)\n\nfor pair in significant_pairs:\n    tech = pair['technician_id']\n    product = pair['product_id']\n    for wo, batch_ids in pair['rejected_by_wo'].items():\n        for bid in batch_ids:\n            tech_total_rejected[tech].append({\n                'batch_id': bid,\n                'work_order_id': wo,\n                'product_id': product\n            })\n    tech_significant_products[tech].add(product)\n\n# Confirmation: >= 3 distinct rejected batches across >= 2 distinct work orders\nconfirmed_techs = set()\ntech_summary = {}\nfor tech, records in tech_total_rejected.items():\n    n_batches = len(records)\n    n_wos = len(set(r['work_order_id'] for r in records))\n    tech_summary[tech] = {'n_batches': n_batches, 'n_wos': n_wos}\n    if n_batches >= 3 and n_wos >= 2:\n        confirmed_techs.add(tech)\n\nprint(\"\\n--- Technician Summary (significant at tech-product level) ---\")\nfor tech in sorted(tech_summary.keys()):\n    s = tech_summary[tech]\n    status = \"CONFIRMED\" if tech in confirmed_techs else \"NOT CONFIRMED\"\n    print(f\"  {tech}: {s['n_batches']} batches, {s['n_wos']} WOs - {status}\")\n\nprint(f\"\\nTotal confirmed technicians: {len(confirmed_techs)}\")\n\n# For confirmed technicians, list affected products\n# A product is included if the (tech, product) pair is significant AND the tech is confirmed\nproduct_to_techs = defaultdict(set)\nfor pair in significant_pairs:\n    tech = pair['technician_id']\n    if tech in confirmed_techs:\n        product_to_techs[pair['product_id']].add(tech)\n\n# Build answer\nanswer = {}\nfor product_id in sorted(product_to_techs.keys()):\n    techs = sorted(product_to_techs[product_id])\n    answer[product_id] = techs\n\nprint(\"\\n=== ANSWER ===\")\nprint(json.dumps(answer, indent=2))\n\n# Detailed view per confirmed tech\nprint(\"\\n--- Detailed confirmed tech analysis ---\")\nfor tech in sorted(confirmed_techs):\n    s = tech_summary[tech]\n    print(f\"\\n{tech}: {s['n_batches']} batches, {s['n_wos']} WOs\")\n    # Show per-product pair stats\n    tech_pairs = [p for p in significant_pairs if p['technician_id'] == tech]\n    for pair in sorted(tech_pairs, key=lambda x: x['pval']):\n        wos = list(pair['rejected_by_wo'].keys())\n        total_k = pair['total_k']\n        total_n = pair['total_n']\n        print(f\"  Product {pair['product_id']}: {total_k}/{total_n} ({total_k/total_n:.0%}) \"\n              f\"vs {pair['baseline']:.2%} baseline, {pair['fold_change']:.1f}x fold, \"\n              f\"p={pair['pval']:.6f}, WOs: {wos}\")\n\nwith open('/app/answer.json', 'w') as f:\n    json.dump(answer, f, indent=2)\nprint(\"\\nSaved to /app/answer.json\")\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false}}

- << 'EOF'\nimport csv, os, json\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\ndef parse_service_reports():\n    events = []\n    base = '/app/raw_service_reports'\n    for day_dir in sorted(os.listdir(base)):\n        day_path = os.path.join(base, day_dir)\n        if not os.path.isdir(day_path):\n            continue\n        for fname in sorted(os.listdir(day_path)):\n            fpath = os.path.join(day_path, fname)\n            with open(fpath) as f:\n                content = f.read()\n            data = {}\n            for line in content.strip().splitlines():\n                if ':' in line:\n                    k, _, v = line.partition(':')\n                    data[k.strip()] = v.strip()\n            completed_str = data.get('COMPLETED', '')\n            h, m = completed_str.split(':')\n            events.append({'work_order_id': data.get('WORK ORDER', ''),\n                          'machine_id': data.get('MACHINE', ''),\n                          'technician_id': data.get('TECHNICIAN', ''),\n                          'day': int(data.get('DAY', 0)),\n                          'completed_min': int(h)*60 + int(m)})\n    return events\n\ndef tmin(t):\n    h, m = t.split(':'); return int(h)*60 + int(m)\n\ndef binom_pvalue(k, n, p):\n    if k <= 0: return 1.0\n    result, coeff = 0.0, 1.0\n    for i in range(n+1):\n        if i > 0: coeff *= (n - i + 1) / i\n        term = coeff * (p**i) * ((1-p)**(n-i))\n        if i >= k: result += term\n    return min(result, 1.0)\n\nservice_events = parse_service_reports()\nmal = {}\nwith open('/app/machine_assignment_log.csv') as f:\n    for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n        mal[row['job_id']] = {'day': int(row['day']), 'machine_id': row['machine_id'],\n                               'start_min': tmin(row['start_time'])}\n\nbatches = []\nwith open('/app/production_batches.csv') as f:\n    for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n        ji = mal.get(row['job_id'], {})\n        batches.append({'batch_id': row['batch_id'], 'machine_id': row['machine_id'],\n                       'product_id': row['product_id'], 'production_day': int(row['production_day']),\n                       'start_min': ji.get('start_min', 0)})\n\ninspections = {}\nwith open('/app/quality_inspections.csv') as f:\n    for row in csv.DictReader(f):\n        inspections[row['batch_id']] = row['inspection_result']\n\nproduct_total = defaultdict(int)\nproduct_rejected = defaultdict(int)\nfor b in batches:\n    product_total[b['product_id']] += 1\n    if inspections.get(b['batch_id']) == 'REJECTED':\n        product_rejected[b['product_id']] += 1\nproduct_baseline = {p: product_rejected[p]/product_total[p] for p in product_total}\n\nmachine_maintenance = defaultdict(list)\nfor ev in service_events:\n    machine_maintenance[ev['machine_id']].append(ev)\nfor mid in machine_maintenance:\n    machine_maintenance[mid].sort(key=lambda x: (x['day'], x['completed_min']))\n\nmachine_windows = {}\nfor machine_id, events in machine_maintenance.items():\n    machine_windows[machine_id] = []\n    for i, ev in enumerate(events):\n        next_ev = events[i+1] if i+1 < len(events) else None\n        machine_windows[machine_id].append({'event': ev, 'next_event': next_ev, 'batches': [], 'rejected_batches': []})\n\nfor batch in batches:\n    mid = batch['machine_id']\n    if mid not in machine_windows: continue\n    batch_time = (batch['production_day'], batch['start_min'])\n    assigned = None\n    for i, win in enumerate(machine_windows[mid]):\n        ev_time = (win['event']['day'], win['event']['completed_min'])\n        if batch_time >= ev_time:\n            if win['next_event'] is None:\n                assigned = i\n            else:\n                nev_time = (win['next_event']['day'], win['next_event']['completed_min'])\n                if batch_time < nev_time:\n                    assigned = i\n    if assigned is not None:\n        win = machine_windows[mid][assigned]\n        win['batches'].append(batch)\n        if inspections.get(batch['batch_id']) == 'REJECTED':\n            win['rejected_batches'].append(batch)\n\n# Per-window binomial test with p < 0.01\nTHRESHOLD = 0.01\nmaintenance_caused = []  # {batch_id, product_id, tech_id, work_order_id, pval, n, k}\n\nfor machine_id, windows in machine_windows.items():\n    for win in windows:\n        if not win['rejected_batches']: continue\n        ev = win['event']\n        prod_batches = defaultdict(list)\n        prod_rejected = defaultdict(list)\n        for b in win['batches']: prod_batches[b['product_id']].append(b)\n        for b in win['rejected_batches']: prod_rejected[b['product_id']].append(b)\n        for product_id, rej_list in prod_rejected.items():\n            n = len(prod_batches[product_id])\n            k = len(rej_list)\n            p_baseline = product_baseline.get(product_id, 0.01)\n            pval = binom_pvalue(k, n, p_baseline)\n            if pval < THRESHOLD:\n                for b in rej_list:\n                    maintenance_caused.append({\n                        'batch_id': b['batch_id'], 'product_id': product_id,\n                        'machine_id': machine_id, 'technician_id': ev['technician_id'],\n                        'work_order_id': ev['work_order_id'], 'pval': pval, 'n': n, 'k': k\n                    })\n\n# Count per technician\ntech_batches = defaultdict(list)\ntech_work_orders = defaultdict(set)\nfor rec in maintenance_caused:\n    tech = rec['technician_id']\n    tech_batches[tech].append(rec)\n    tech_work_orders[tech].add(rec['work_order_id'])\n\n# Confirm technicians\nconfirmed_techs = set()\nfor tech, recs in tech_batches.items():\n    n_b = len(recs)\n    n_wo = len(tech_work_orders[tech])\n    if n_b >= 3 and n_wo >= 2:\n        confirmed_techs.add(tech)\n\nprint(f\"Confirmed technicians ({len(confirmed_techs)}): {sorted(confirmed_techs)}\")\nprint()\n\n# Detailed breakdown per confirmed tech\nfor tech in sorted(confirmed_techs):\n    n_b = len(tech_batches[tech])\n    n_wo = len(tech_work_orders[tech])\n    print(f\"{tech}: {n_b} batches, {n_wo} WOs\")\n    # Show per WO details\n    wo_prod = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(list))\n    for rec in tech_batches[tech]:\n        wo_prod[rec['work_order_id']][rec['product_id']].append(rec)\n    for wo, prods in sorted(wo_prod.items()):\n        for prod, recs in sorted(prods.items()):\n            r = recs[0]\n            print(f\"  {wo}, {prod}: {r['k']}/{r['n']} (p={r['pval']:.6f}), {len(recs)} batches\")\n    print()\n\n# Products for confirmed techs\nproduct_to_techs = defaultdict(set)\nfor rec in maintenance_caused:\n    if rec['technician_id'] in confirmed_techs:\n        product_to_techs[rec['product_id']].add(rec['technician_id'])\n\nanswer = {p: sorted(t) for p, t in sorted(product_to_techs.items())}\nprint(\"=== FINAL ANSWER ===\")\nprint(json.dumps(answer, indent=2))\n\nwith open('/app/answer.json', 'w') as f:\n    json.dump(answer, f, {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_014ekW8CeZfz6M3LcS3331Kx","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The analysis is complete. Here's a summary of the methodology and findings:\n\n## Methodology\n\n**Step 1 — Linking batches to maintenance windows**: Using precise job start times from `machine_assignment_log.csv` and maintenance completion times from the raw service reports, each batch was assigned to the maintenance window it was produced in (after one maintenance event, before the next on the same machine).\n\n**Step 2 — Statistical filtering**: For each (product, maintenance window) pair with rejections, a one-sided binomial test was performed comparing the observed rejection rate to the product's baseline rejection rate across the full dataset. Only pairs with **p < 0.01** were counted as maintenance-caused (this threshold is necessary because each technician has 60–100+ work orders, making borderline 5%-level cases likely false positives by chance alone — borderline-only technicians showed 60–97% false-positive rates).\n\n**Step 3 — Technician confirmation**: A technician was confirmed only with ≥3 distinct maintenance-caused rejected batches across ≥2 different work orders.\n\n**Step 4 — Product attribution**: For each confirmed technician, all products with at least one statistically significant maintenance-caused rejection were reported.\n\n## Results\n\n**10 confirmed technicians** with clear patterns of maintenance-caused quality rejections:\n\n| Technician | Batches | Work Orders | Strongest Evidence |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| TECH_002 | 12 | 7 WOs | P_029: 3/3 same-window rejections |\n| TECH_003 | 8 | 3 WOs | P_004: 3/3, 2/2 rejections |\n| TECH_014 | 24 | 9 WOs | P_015: 5/5, P_026: 3/3 & 2/2, P_001: 2/2 |\n| TECH_021 | 10 | 4 WOs | P_027: 4/4, P_015: 4/4 |\n| TECH_027 | 12 | 4 WOs | P_001: 5/5, 3/3 rejections |\n| TECH_030 | 5 | 2 WOs | P_019: 3/3, P_004: 2/2 |\n| TECH_031 | 10 | 5 WOs | P_008: 3/3 (×2 WOs) |\n| TECH_032 | 6 | 4 WOs | P_006: 2/3, P_010: 2/3 |\n| TECH_037 | 4 | 3 WOs | P_024: 2/2 |\n| TECH_041 | 4 | 2 WOs | P_010: 2/2, P_024: 2/2 |"}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1013,"cache_read_input_tokens":126248,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1013,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"7eb1499c-650f-4ac9-a026-11d01c0514ec","uuid":"0b7a575f-d247-4229-9321-c50447ff10b9","request_id":"req_011CbfCxPSa3bDMSgWrtYHym"}
